WanVideoBlockList.description: Comma separated list of blocks to apply block swap to, can also use ranges like '0-5' or '0,2,3-5' etc., can be connected to the dense_blocks input of 'WanVideoSetRadialAttention' node
WanVideoBlockList.name: WanVideoBlockList

WanVideoBlockSwap.description: Settings for block swapping, reduces VRAM use by swapping blocks to CPU memory

block_swap_debug.tooltip: Enable debug logging for block swapping

prefetch_blocks.tooltip: Number of blocks to prefetch ahead, can speed up processing but increases memory usage. 1 is usually enough to offset speed loss from block swapping, use the debug option to confirm it for your system

use_non_blocking.tooltip: Use non-blocking memory transfer for offloading, reserves more RAM but is faster

vace_blocks_to_swap.tooltip: Number of VACE blocks to swap, the VACE model has 15 blocks

blocks_to_swap.tooltip: Number of transformer blocks to swap, the 14B model has 40, while the 1.3B and 5B models have 30 blocks. LongCat-video has 48

WanVideoBlockSwap.name: WanVideoBlockSwap

tiles.tooltip: Use matteo's tiled image encoding for improved accuracy

combine_embeds.tooltip: Method to combine multiple clip embeds

WanVideoContextOptions.description: Context options for WanVideo, allows splitting the video into context windows and attemps blending them for longer generations than the model and memory otherwise would allow.

fuse_method.tooltip: Window weight function: linear=ramps at edges only, pyramid=triangular weights peaking in middle

reference_latent.tooltip: Image to be used as init for I2V models for windows where first frame is not the actual first frame. Mostly useful with MAGREF model

context_frames.tooltip: Number of pixel frames in the context, NOTE: the latent space has 4 frames in 1

context_overlap.tooltip: Context overlap as pixel frames, NOTE: the latent space has 4 frames in 1

context_stride.tooltip: Context stride as pixel frames, NOTE: the latent space has 4 frames in 1

WanVideoContextOptions.name: WanVideoContextOptions

end_percent.tooltip: End percent of the control signal

control_start_percent.tooltip: Start percent of the steps to apply controlnet

load_device.tooltip: Initial device to load the model to, NOT recommended with the larger models unless you have 48GB+ VRAM

model.tooltip: These models are loaded from the 'ComfyUI/models/controlnet' -folder

enable_vae_tiling.tooltip: Drastically reduces memory use but will introduce seams at tile stride boundaries. The location and number of seams is dictated by the tile stride size. The visibility of seams can be controlled by increasing the tile size. Seams become less obvious at 1.5x stride and are barely noticeable at 2x stride size. Which is to say if you use a stride width of 160, the seams are barely noticeable with a tile width of 320.

tile_stride_x.tooltip: Tile stride width in pixels. Smaller values use less VRAM but will introduce more seams.

tile_stride_y.tooltip: Tile stride height in pixels. Smaller values use less VRAM but will introduce more seams.

tile_x.tooltip: Tile width in pixels. Smaller values use less VRAM but will make seams more obvious.

tile_y.tooltip: Tile height in pixels. Smaller values use less VRAM but will make seams more obvious.

rope_function.tooltip: Comfy's RoPE implementation doesn't use complex numbers and can thus be compiled, that should be a lot faster when using torch.compile

samples.tooltip: init Latents to use for video2video process

addnoise_condition.tooltip: Improves consistency in long video generation

force_offload.tooltip: Moves the model to the offload device after sampling

easycache_thresh.tooltip: How strongly to cache the output of diffusion model. This value must be non-negative.

extra_latents.tooltip: First latent to use for the Pusa -model

height.tooltip: Height of the image to encode

width.tooltip: Width of the image to encode

length.tooltip: Length of the audio latent sequence

latent_strength.tooltip: Additional latent multiplier, helpful for leapfusion I2V where lower values allow for more motion

noise_aug_strength.tooltip: Strength of noise augmentation, helpful for leapfusion I2V where some noise can add motion and give sharper results

enable_vae_tiling.tooltip: Drastically reduces memory use but may introduce seams

tile_stride_x.tooltip: Tile stride in pixels, smaller values use less VRAM, may introduce more seams

tile_stride_y.tooltip: Tile stride in pixels, smaller values use less VRAM, may introduce more seams

tile_x.tooltip: Tile size in pixels, smaller values use less VRAM, may introduce more seams

tile_y.tooltip: Tile size in pixels, smaller values use less VRAM, may introduce more seams

weight.tooltip: The feta Weight of the Enhance-A-Video

bidirectional_sampling.tooltip: Enable bidirectional sampling, based on https://github.com/ff2416/WanFM

raag_alpha.tooltip: Alpha value for RAAG, 1.0 is default, 0.0 is disabled.

tsr_sigma.tooltip: How early TSR steer the sampling process

use_tcfg.tooltip: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.18137 TCFG: Tangential Damping Classifier-free Guidance. CFG artifacts reduction.

video_attention_split_steps.tooltip: Steps to split self attention when using multiple prompts

zero_star_steps.tooltip: Steps to split self attention when using multiple prompts

extra_latents.tooltip: Extra latents to add to the input front, used for Skyreels A2 reference images

fun_or_fl2v_model.tooltip: Enable when using official FLF2V or Fun model

tiled_vae.tooltip: Use tiled VAE encoding for reduced memory use

end_latent_strength.tooltip: Additional latent multiplier, helpful for I2V where lower values allow for more motion

WanVideoImageToVideoMultiTalk.description: Enables Multi/InfiniteTalk long video generation sampling method, the video is created in windows with overlapping frames. Not compatible or necessary to be used with context windows and many other features besides Multi/InfiniteTalk.

mode.tooltip: The sampling strategy to use in the long video generation loop, should match the model used

output_path.tooltip: If set, will save each window's resulting frames to this folder, also DISABLES returning the final video tensor to save memory

tiled_vae.tooltip: Use tiled VAE encoding for reduced memory use

colormatch.tooltip: Color matching method to use between the windows

force_offload.tooltip: Whether to force offload the model within the loop for VAE operations, enable if you encounter memory issues.

frame_window_size.tooltip: The number of frames to process at once, should be a value the model is generally good at.

motion_frame.tooltip: Driven frame length used in the long video generation. Basically the overlap length.

WanVideoImageToVideoMultiTalk.name: WanVideoImageToVideoMultiTalk

WanVideoImageToVideoSkyreelsv3_audio.description: Enables Multi/InfiniteTalk long video generation sampling method, the video is created in windows with overlapping frames. Not compatible or necessary to be used with context windows and many other features besides Multi/InfiniteTalk.

output_path.tooltip: If set, will save each window's resulting frames to this folder, also DISABLES returning the final video tensor to save memory

reference_video.tooltip: Optional: Pre-generated reference video to use for keyframes instead of extracting from first generation. Should be color-matched to source image.

colormatch.tooltip: Color matching method to use between the windows

drop_frames.tooltip: Additional frames to drop when advancing the audio window. Higher values = less overlap = faster generation but potentially less smooth transitions.

force_offload.tooltip: Whether to force offload the model within the loop for VAE operations, enable if you encounter memory issues.

frame_window_size.tooltip: The number of frames to process at once, should be a value the model is generally good at.

motion_frame.tooltip: Driven frame length used in the long video generation. Basically the overlap length.

tiled_vae.tooltip: Use tiled VAE encoding for reduced memory use

WanVideoImageToVideoSkyreelsv3_audio.name: WanVideoImageToVideoSkyreelsv3_audio

WanVideoLatentReScale.description: Rescale latents to match the expected range for encoding or decoding between native ComfyUI VAE and the WanVideoWrapper VAE.

direction.tooltip: Direction to rescale latents, from comfy to wrapper or vice versa

WanVideoLatentReScale.name: WanVideoLatentReScale

ref_latent.tooltip: Reference latent used for consistency, generally should be either the init image, or first latent from first generation

samples.tooltip: For the sampler 'samples' input, used for slicing samples per window for vid2vid

frames_processed.tooltip: Number of frames already processed in the video, used to select audio features

if_not_enough_audio.tooltip: What to do if there are not enough frames in pose_images for the window

num_frames.tooltip: Number of new frames to generate

overlap.tooltip: Number of overlapping frames from previous latents for video continuation, set to 0 for T2V

prev_latents.tooltip: Full previous latents to be used to continue generation, continuation frames are selected based on 'overlap' parameter

ref_frame_index.tooltip: Values between 0 - 24 ensures better consistency, while selecting other ranges (e.g., -10 or 30) helps reduce repeated actions

ref_mask_frame_range.tooltip: Larger range can further help mitigate repeated actions, but excessively large values may introduce artifacts

merge_loras.tooltip: Merge LoRAs into the model, otherwise they are loaded on the fly. Always disabled for GGUF and scaled fp8 models. This affects ALL LoRAs, not just the current one

lora.tooltip: LORA models are expected to be in ComfyUI/models/loras with .safetensors extension

strength.tooltip: LORA strength, set to 0.0 to unmerge the LORA

vram_management_args.tooltip: Alternative offloading method from DiffSynth-Studio, more aggressive in reducing memory use than block swapping, but can be slower

quantization.tooltip: Optional quantization method, 'disabled' acts as autoselect based by weights. Scaled modes only work with matching weights, _fast modes (fp8 matmul) require CUDA compute capability >= 8.9 (NVIDIA 4000 series and up), e4m3fn generally can not be torch.compiled on compute capability < 8.9 (3000 series and under)

steps.tooltip: Number of steps for the scheduler
Number of steps for the scheduler

WanVideoSetAttentionModeOverride.description: Override the attention mode for the model for specific step and/or block range

end_step.tooltip: Step to end applying the attention mode override

start_step.tooltip: Step to start applying the attention mode override

verbose.tooltip: Print verbose info about attention mode override during generation

WanVideoSetAttentionModeOverride.name: WanVideoSetAttentionModeOverride

WanVideoSetLoRAs.description: Sets the LoRA weights to be used directly in linear layers of the model, this does NOT merge LoRAs
WanVideoSetLoRAs.name: WanVideoSetLoRAs

WanVideoSetRadialAttention.description: Sets radial attention parameters, dense attention refers to normal attention

block_size.tooltip: Radial attention block size, larger blocks are faster but restricts usable dimensions more.

decay_factor.tooltip: Controls how quickly the attention window shrinks as the distance between frames increases in the sparse attention mask.

dense_attention_mode.tooltip: The attention mode for dense attention

dense_blocks.tooltip: Number of blocks to apply normal attention to

dense_timesteps.tooltip: The step to start applying sparse attention

dense_vace_blocks.tooltip: Number of vace blocks to apply normal attention to

WanVideoSetRadialAttention.name: WanVideoSetRadialAttention

WanVideoSigmaToStep.description: Simply passes a float value as an integer, used to set start/end steps with sigma threshold
WanVideoSigmaToStep.name: WanVideoSigmaToStep

WanVideoTeaCache.description:
Patch WanVideo model to use TeaCache. Speeds up inference by caching the output and  
applying it instead of doing the step.  Best results are achieved by choosing the  
appropriate coefficients for the model. Early steps should never be skipped, with too  
aggressive values this can happen and the motion suffers. Starting later can help with that too.   
When NOT using coefficients, the threshold value should be  
about 10 times smaller than the value used with coefficients.  

Official recommended values https://github.com/ali-vilab/TeaCache/tree/main/TeaCache4Wan2.1

mode.tooltip: Choice between using e (time embeds, default) or e0 (modulated time embeds)

rel_l1_thresh.tooltip: Higher values will make TeaCache more aggressive, faster, but may cause artifacts. Good value range for 1.3B: 0.05 - 0.08, for other models 0.15-0.30

start_step.tooltip: Start percentage of the steps to apply TeaCache

use_coefficients.tooltip: Use calculated coefficients for more accuracy. When enabled therel_l1_thresh should be about 10 times higher than without

WanVideoTeaCache.name: WanVideoTeaCache

WanVideoTextEmbedBridge.description: Bridge between ComfyUI native text embedding and WanVideoWrapper text embedding
WanVideoTextEmbedBridge.name: WanVideoTextEmbedBridge

WanVideoTextEncode.description: Encodes text prompts into text embeddings. For rudimentary prompt travel you can input multiple prompts separated by '|', they will be equally spread over the video length

device.tooltip: Device to run the text encoding on.

model_to_offload.tooltip: Model to move to offload_device before encoding

use_disk_cache.tooltip: Cache the text embeddings to disk for faster re-use, under the custom_nodes/ComfyUI-WanVideoWrapper/text_embed_cache directory

WanVideoTextEncode.name: WanVideoTextEncode

WanVideoTextEncodeCached.description:
Encodes text prompts into text embeddings. This node loads and completely unloads the T5 after done,  
leaving no VRAM or RAM imprint. If prompts have been cached before T5 is not loaded at all.  
negative output is meant to be used with NAG, it contains only negative prompt embeddings.  

Additionally you can provide a Qwen LLM model to extend the positive prompt with either one  
of the original Wan templates or a custom system prompt.

extender_args.tooltip: Use this node to extend the prompt with additional text.

device.tooltip: Device to run the text encoding on.

use_disk_cache.tooltip: Cache the text embeddings to disk for faster re-use, under the custom_nodes/ComfyUI-WanVideoWrapper/text_embed_cache directory

WanVideoTextEncodeCached.name: WanVideoTextEncodeCached

WanVideoTextEncodeSingle.description: Encodes text prompt into text embedding.

device.tooltip: Device to run the text encoding on.

model_to_offload.tooltip: Model to move to offload_device before encoding

use_disk_cache.tooltip: Cache the text embeddings to disk for faster re-use, under the custom_nodes/ComfyUI-WanVideoWrapper/text_embed_cache directory

WanVideoTextEncodeSingle.name: WanVideoTextEncodeSingle

parallel.tooltip: uses more memory but is faster

WanVideoTorchCompileSettings.description: torch.compile settings, when connected to the model loader, torch.compile of the selected layers is attempted. Requires Triton and torch > 2.7.0 is recommended

allow_unmerged_lora_compile.tooltip: Allow LoRA application to be compiled with torch.compile to avoid graph breaks, causes issues with some LoRAs, mostly dynamic ones

compile_transformer_blocks_only.tooltip: Compile only the transformer blocks, usually enough and can make compilation faster and less error prone

WanVideoTorchCompileSettings.name: WanVideoTorchCompileSettings

WanVideoUltraVicoSettings.description: Set UltraVico parameters, attention mode still needs to be set to sageattn_ultravico, https://github.com/thu-ml/DiT-Extrapolation

alpha.tooltip: Alpha value for the decay, higher values mean slower decay

WanVideoUltraVicoSettings.name: WanVideoUltraVicoSettings

load_device.tooltip: Initial device to load the model to, NOT recommended with the larger models unless you have 48GB+ VRAM

model.tooltip: These models are loaded from the 'ComfyUI/models/controlnet' -folder

offload.tooltip: If enabled, the controlnet model will be offloaded before main model block processing to save VRAM.

end_percent.tooltip: End percent of the steps to apply the controlnet

start_percent.tooltip: Start percent of the steps to apply the controlnet

colorspace.tooltip: Color space for the output image

handle_not_detected.tooltip: How to handle undetected poses, empty inserts black and repeat inserts previous detection

end_percent.tooltip: End percentage for the pose control

start_percent.tooltip: Start percentage for the pose control

height.tooltip: Height of the image to encode

width.tooltip: Width of the image to encode

tiled_vae.tooltip: Use tiled VAE encoding for reduced memory use

height.tooltip: Height of the image to encode

vace_end_percent.tooltip: End percent of the steps to apply VACE

vace_start_percent.tooltip: Start percent of the steps to apply VACE

width.tooltip: Width of the image to encode

vace_model.tooltip: These models are loaded from the 'ComfyUI/models/diffusion_models' VACE model to use when not using model that has it included

verbose.tooltip: Enables memory usage logging when using the model

line_resolution.tooltip: Number of points to use for each line segment
